subject: Geology
title: Soil From Rock
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Soil From Rock.

hook.text: Every farm on Earth grows food in broken rock. Soil is just weathered minerals mixed with organic matter.

buildup.text: Soil forms when rocks break down through weathering and mix with decayed plants, animals, and microbes.

discovery.text: It takes about 500 years to form one inch of topsoil. Erosion can destroy it in a single storm.

twist.text: The type of bedrock determines soil chemistry. Limestone soil differs from granite soil, affecting what grows.

climax.text: Soil is where geology meets life. Without rock weathering, there would be no soil and no terrestrial ecosystems.

punchline.text: All life on land begins with broken rock.

quiz.question: How long does it take to form one inch of topsoil?
